jokes.text: thing-a-ding-ding
jokes.setup: Character is trying to euphemistically describe a penis-shaped cake
jokes.punchline: The childish, ridiculous term 'thing-a-ding-ding'
jokes.explanation: Childish euphemism gets mild laughs through Monica's discomfort with saying 'penis'

jokes.text: put eyes on the 'hoojies' and we'll just say it's just some sort of weird elephant
jokes.setup: Trying to salvage an inappropriate penis cake
jokes.punchline: The absurd attempt to disguise it as an elephant by adding eyes
jokes.explanation: Creative visual absurdity - the mental image of a penis cake with eyes pretending to be an elephant is genuinely funny

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Too soon. You go.
jokes.setup: Monica gets excited about Joey's announcement before hearing what it is
jokes.punchline: Her embarrassed realization she jumped the gun
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Monica's eagerness, economically delivered

jokes.text: Is that something you're making up? / No, no, no, it's real! And it has been, since 1998.
jokes.setup: Skepticism about the legitimacy of a 'Soapie' award
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive response that it's been real for the whopping 2-3 years
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline about the absurd newness of this 'prestigious' award

jokes.text: write 'NASA' on the side and then give it to Ross
jokes.setup: Still trying to salvage the penis cake
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea that writing 'NASA' will make a penis cake appropriate for Ross
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalating absurdity of trying to make the cake appropriate

jokes.text: That's the third most prestigious soap opera award there is!
jokes.setup: Joey's excitement about winning a Soapie
jokes.punchline: Rachel's backhanded 'compliment' about it being third most prestigious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel delivery - supportive but unintentionally deflating. Great economy and character voice

jokes.text: You're getting married. This is all I have.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting to go to the awards ceremony
jokes.punchline: Joey's pathetic but honest admission about his life priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Joey's character - simultaneously funny and genuinely touching in its honesty

jokes.text: With two tennis balls.
jokes.setup: Offering to get Joey a microphone-shaped cake
jokes.punchline: The visual of tennis balls completing the phallic imagery
jokes.explanation: Obvious penis joke but gets a laugh through Monica's obliviousness

jokes.text: But in my head, it sounded something like this: [dinosaur noise]
jokes.setup: Student asking what noise a velociraptor would make
jokes.punchline: Ross making an elaborate dinosaur screech in class
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross - his dinosaur obsession leading to an embarrassing public moment. Perfect character integration

jokes.text: What did I get? / Seven.
jokes.setup: Student asking about his failing grade
jokes.punchline: The shockingly low score of 7 out of 100
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The single word 'Seven' is devastatingly funny in its understatement

jokes.text: That brings me in the loop a little.
jokes.setup: Student confessing he's in love with Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's hilariously understated response to this bombshell
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - Ross treating a love confession like a minor detail that clarifies things. Perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: In spite of what may be written on the backs of these chairs.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining he likes women
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to homophobic graffiti on university chairs
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about university life, delivered with Ross's characteristic awkwardness

jokes.text: But if Chandler's in an accident and can't perform sexually... he'd want you to take a lover to satisfy needs he'd no longer fulfill.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she's engaged when hit on
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarrely specific and morbid hypothetical justification
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - absurdly elaborate logic delivered completely seriously. Perfect character voice and originality

jokes.text: You should be embarrassed.
jokes.setup: Guy saying he's embarrassed about approaching the wrong woman
jokes.punchline: Monica's cutting response after being rejected
jokes.explanation: Good defensive reaction from Monica - wounded pride delivered sharply

jokes.text: I thought you knew I looked at you. / I did, but that was really fun.
jokes.setup: Monica being confused about who the guy was interested in
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admitting she knew but enjoyed the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Phoebe's mischievous nature and Monica's competitiveness

jokes.text: I'm fourth?
jokes.setup: Joey practicing his acceptance speech and listing friends
jokes.punchline: Ross's wounded reaction to being listed fourth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross reaction - petty concern about ranking among friends. Great economy

jokes.text: No big deal. I do that with my shampoo bottle. / Really? / Yeah. / What award are you practicing for? / Grammy.
jokes.setup: Chandler admitting he practices acceptance speeches too
jokes.punchline: The absurd escalation to Grammy for Best New Artist
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from relatable behavior to absurd ambition. Good character work for both

jokes.text: You're up against the guy who survived his own cremation.
jokes.setup: Discussing Joey's tough category
jokes.punchline: The hilariously absurd soap opera plot point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant one-liner capturing the absurdity of soap opera storytelling

jokes.text: You practice losing the Grammys too? / No, at the Grammys, I always win.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about Chandler's Grammy fantasies
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact confidence about winning Grammys
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Chandler's characteristic deadpan confidence in his imaginary scenarios

jokes.text: What's your dog's name again? / Joey.
jokes.setup: Waitress asking about Monica's dog
jokes.punchline: Monica naming her 'dog' after Joey, implying she's taking leftovers for him
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke with perfect economy. Simple but effective

jokes.text: I feel like I've had 10 drinks today. I've only had six.
jokes.setup: Monica describing feeling intoxicated by new love
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's actually had six actual drinks
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up romantic metaphor then reveals literal drinking

jokes.text: No, a guy would say, 'I'll never get to sleep with anyone else.' / I'll never sleep with anyone else!
jokes.setup: Monica distinguishing how men vs women think about marriage
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself by having the 'male' thought
jokes.explanation: Good self-contradiction joke showing Monica's realization hitting in real time

jokes.text: Whoa, what? / Ross has a boyfriend!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining about a student with a crush
jokes.punchline: Monica's loud misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding joke - gets a laugh but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Not right now.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if he gives off a sexy professor vibe
jokes.punchline: Monica's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica response - blunt honesty delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: How'd you get over that teacher? / I didn't. I got under him.
jokes.setup: Ross asking how Monica got over her teacher crush
jokes.punchline: The sexual wordplay of 'under' vs 'over'
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline wordplay. Well-executed if predictable sexual humor

jokes.text: Bye! / Bye! / I'll miss you! / I'll miss you too!
jokes.setup: Normal goodbye between new couple
jokes.punchline: The escalating clinginess of multiple goodbyes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing new relationship clinginess, relatable and mildly cringey

jokes.text: Pull my finger.
jokes.setup: Monica talking about their deep, meaningful relationship
jokes.punchline: Chandler's juvenile request completely undermining the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation - juvenile humor perfectly timed to undermine romantic talk

jokes.text: I was in the bathroom, and Susan Lucci came out of a stall... and she had toilet paper on her shoe, so I took it.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she was late
jokes.punchline: The bizarre celebrity toilet paper trophy
jokes.explanation: Delightfully absurd and perfectly in character - Rachel's celebrity obsession in weird form

jokes.text: Can I squeeze your ass? / On TV? / Yeah!
jokes.setup: Joey asking about hugging if he wins
jokes.punchline: His excitement about inappropriate touching being broadcast
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Joey - inappropriate but enthusiastic about everything

jokes.text: What the...?!
jokes.setup: Joey losing the award
jokes.punchline: His shocked, incomplete curse reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction shot - gets sympathy laugh but nothing special

jokes.text: Unfortunately, Jessica couldn't be with us tonight... so I'll accept this award on her behalf.
jokes.setup: Joey presenting an award to his co-star
jokes.punchline: Immediately deciding to steal the award
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Joey's immediate pivot from presenter to thief is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Sure I do. It's a verb. As in 'I be half-in' it'!
jokes.setup: Being told he doesn't know what 'behalf' means
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident but completely wrong etymology
jokes.explanation: Peak Joey stupidity delivered with complete confidence. Perfect character integration and quotability

jokes.text: It could also be a Grammy.
jokes.setup: Joey defending keeping the stolen award
jokes.punchline: His delusional belief it could pass for a Grammy
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting Joey's Grammy fantasies to his current theft

jokes.text: Joey, it says 'Best Supporting Actress.' / I can scratch that right off.
jokes.setup: Monica pointing out the award says 'Actress'
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual solution to scratch it off
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's delusion - his matter-of-fact approach to fraud

jokes.text: If I can't have it, you can't have it! [breaks award]
jokes.setup: Being convinced to return the award
jokes.punchline: Joey's petulant destruction of the award
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat - childish tantrum escalating the situation

jokes.text: What? / He's totally yanking your chain. He's done this with three other teachers.
jokes.setup: Ross believing the student's feelings are real
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a serial scam
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist that makes Ross look gullible

jokes.text: I love you. / All right. / What? It's true.
jokes.setup: Lewis being exposed as a fake
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the lie even when caught
jokes.explanation: Weak follow-up to the revelation - doesn't add much beyond awkwardness

jokes.text: All right, fine. You love me so much, come over here and give me a kiss.
jokes.setup: Student insisting he really loves Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross calling his bluff with physical challenge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Ross getting fed up and calling the bluff

jokes.text: You got an 80! You didn't even fail! Have some self-respect!
jokes.setup: The second student's love confession
jokes.punchline: Revealing he actually passed and is just greedy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and Ross's indignant delivery. Good callback structure

jokes.text: When you have doubts or fears or anxieties about a relationship... don't communicate them to your husband.
jokes.setup: Monica asking for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's hilariously bad advice about suppressing communication
jokes.explanation: Good subversive humor - awful advice delivered as wisdom

jokes.text: The woman who married a bunch of times and killed herself when you were 13?
jokes.setup: Phoebe citing her mother's advice
jokes.punchline: Monica's brutal assessment of the source
jokes.explanation: Brutally funny reality check - dark humor about Phoebe's tragic backstory

jokes.text: Does this also mean that putting out doesn't get you love?
jokes.setup: Realizing her mother's advice was wrong
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent question about another piece of bad advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Phoebe's innocent delivery of inappropriate content

jokes.text: No, I try to save that for real awards.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if she'll display the award
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of the Soapie as not a 'real' award
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Joey's pride - casual dismissal is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: No, when I first meet somebody... it's mostly panic, anxiety and a great deal of sweating.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he gets excited meeting new people
jokes.punchline: Chandler's brutally honest assessment of his social anxiety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - self-deprecating and neurotic but relatable

jokes.text: I was dangerously dehydrated the first six months of our relationship.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he sweats even with her
jokes.punchline: The absurd extension of his sweating problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and callback - absurd but perfectly in character for Chandler

jokes.text: Besides, you know what? I'll have a lot of new things with you. The first time we buy a house. Our first kid. Our first grandkid.
jokes.setup: Monica worrying about no new experiences
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sweet but overwhelming list of future firsts
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with mild comedy from Chandler's earnest over-planning

jokes.text: Water. Water. Water.
jokes.setup: Monica getting emotional about their future
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panic response needing hydration
jokes.explanation: Good callback and perfect Chandler panic response with great economy

jokes.text: Did you get a little haircut? / Yeah, yeah. Do you like it? Do you love it?
jokes.setup: Student trying to flirt with Ross again
jokes.punchline: Ross's desperate need for validation about his haircut
jokes.explanation: Mild callback showing Ross's insecurity even while trying to be stern

jokes.text: You used me! You don't love me and you never did!
jokes.setup: Ross confronting the student about the scam
jokes.punchline: His overly emotional reaction like a spurned lover
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Ross treating academic fraud like romantic betrayal

jokes.text: And, you know, to win one for a massage! Especially just after winning a Tony Award for Best Actress... in Reservations at Eight by Neil Simon.
jokes.setup: Phoebe practicing acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: The absurd escalation from Nobel Prize to Tony to made-up Neil Simon play
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity and perfect Phoebe delusion of grandeur

jokes.text: Live from New York, it's Saturday Night!
jokes.setup: Continuing her acceptance speech practice
jokes.punchline: Ending with SNL catchphrase instead of award speech
jokes.explanation: Good end to the speech montage with familiar reference
